subject: Physics
solution:
Given, ux=5 m s-1, ax=3 m s-2, x=84 m

The formula to calculate the velocity of the particle along x-axis is given by

vx2=ux2+2ax   ...1

From equation (1), it follows that

vx2=25+2(3)(84)⇒vx=23 m s-1

Also, the velocity of the particle along x-axis can be written as

vx=ux+axt   ...2

From equation (2), it follows that

t=23-53=6 s

Similarly, for the y- component of the velocity, it follows that

vy=0+ayt=0+2×(6)=12 m s-1

Hence, the magnitude of the velocity is given by

v2=vx2+vy2=232+122=673v=673 m s-1

Therefore, α=673.

So, the average velocity is 

vavg=3R2R3=332

=1.53 m s-1

solution:
Given the instantaneous position is

x=t3-6t2+20t+15   ...1

The velocity of the particle is given by

v=dxdt=3t2-12t+20   ...2

And, the acceleration of the particle is given by

a=dvdt=6t-12   ...3

When a=0, it implies

6t-12=0⇒t=2 s

At t=2 s, the velocity can be calculated as follows:

v=3(2)2-12(2)+20=8 m s-1

solution:
The time t1 taken by the person to travel distance x with velocity v1 is given by

t1=xv1   ...1

The time t2 taken by the person to travel distance x with velocity v2 is given by

t2=xv2   ...2

The formula to calculate the average speed v of the person is given by

v=2xt1+t2   ...3

Substitute the expressions from equations (1) and (2) into equation (3) to obtain the required relation.

v=2xxv1+xv2⇒xv1+xv2=2xv⇒2v=1v1+1v2

subject: Physics
solution:
The velocity vector is given by $\vec{v} = v_x \hat{i} + v_y \hat{j} + v_z \hat{k} = -x \hat{i} + 2y \hat{j} - z \hat{k}$.

The components of acceleration are:

$a_x = v_x \dfrac{\partial v_x}{\partial x} + v_y \dfrac{\partial v_x}{\partial y} + v_z \dfrac{\partial v_x}{\partial z} = (-x)(-1) + 0 + 0 = x$

$a_y = v_x \dfrac{\partial v_y}{\partial x} + v_y \dfrac{\partial v_y}{\partial y} + v_z \dfrac{\partial v_y}{\partial z} = 0 + (2y)(2) + 0 = 4y$

$a_z = v_x \dfrac{\partial v_z}{\partial x} + v_y \dfrac{\partial v_z}{\partial y} + v_z \dfrac{\partial v_z}{\partial z} = 0 + 0 + (-z)(-1) = z$

The acceleration vector is $\vec{a} = x \hat{i} + 4y \hat{j} + z \hat{k}$.

At the point $(1, 2, 4)$, substituting $x = 1$, $y = 2$, and $z = 4$:

$\vec{a} = 1 \hat{i} + 4(2) \hat{j} + 4 \hat{k} = \hat{i} + 8 \hat{j} + 4 \hat{k}$

The magnitude of acceleration is:

$|\vec{a}| = \sqrt{1^2 + 8^2 + 4^2} = \sqrt{1 + 64 + 16} = \sqrt{81} = 9$ m/s$^2$

Answer: $9$

subject: Physics
solution:
Let total distance travelled by horse rider be 2x.

Now, the time taken to cover first half distance is tAB=x5

Second half distance x between BC is covered in two parts, x=d1+d2

here d1 is the distance travelled with 10  m s-1 and d2 is the distance travelled with 15 m s-1 in equal time intervals t2 each.

Then, d1=10t2, d2=15t2

Second half distance x=t2(10+15)=25t2

Now, the mean speed of rider is v=2xx5+2x25=2×255+2=507  m s-1

Thus, the value of x=50.

subject: Physics
solution:
The v-x graph shows velocity decreasing linearly with distance. Using kinematic equation $v^2 = v_0^2 + 2ax$, we can establish the relationship between acceleration and position.
From the linear v-x relationship, differentiating using $a = v\frac{dv}{dx}$ yields acceleration that is linearly proportional to distance from the origin.
The a-x graph shows a straight line passing through the origin with positive slope, indicating that acceleration increases linearly with distance while remaining positive throughout the motion.

If t be the required time, it follows that

S1+S2=12at2   ...3

Equations (1), (2) and (3) imply that

(p-1)2+p2=t2⇒t=2p2-2p+1 s

subject: Physics
solution:
The total distance travelled by the particle is equal to the sum of the magnitudes of the areas under the velocity-time graph.

Area of the first triangle (from $t = 0$ to $t = 20\text{ s}$) is:
$A_1 = \dfrac{1}{2} \times 20 \times 5 = 50\text{ m}$

Magnitude of the area of the second triangle (from $t = 20$ to $t = 40\text{ s}$) is:
$|A_2| = \left| \dfrac{1}{2} \times 20 \times (-5) \right| = 50\text{ m}$

Total distance $= A_1 + |A_2| = 50 + 50 = 100\text{ m}$

The total displacement is the algebraic sum of the areas under the velocity-time graph.

Total displacement $= A_1 + A_2 = 50 - 50 = 0\text{ m}$

Average velocity is the ratio of total displacement to total time.

Average velocity $= \dfrac{\text{Total displacement}}{\text{Total time}} = \dfrac{0}{40} = 0$

Answer: $100\text{ m}$ and zero

⇒10+x=12at2×4  

⇒10+x=10×4

  ⇒x=30 m

subject: Physics
solution:
The formula to calculate the velocity of the ball after 3 s is given by

v3=u-3g   ...1

The formula to calculate the velocity of the ball after 5 s is given by

v5=u-5g   ...2

Divide equation (1) by equation (2) to obtain the required ratio.

v3v5=u-3gu-5g   ...3

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (3) to calculate the required ratio.

v3v5=150-30150-50=120100=65= 5+15

Comparing the above equation with the given expression, it can be written that x=5.

When the block will be moving up the incline, the external force will be up the incline and will be balancing the down the incline component of gravity.

When the block will be moving down the incline, the external force will be again up the incline and will be balancing the down the incline component of gravity. But in this case the external force will be on the back side of the block.

F=mgsinθ=2 N
